items.title: ‘Tell Him He’s a Piece of Shit’: Meta’s New AI Unit Is a Total Mess
items.summary: Executives and employees alike are struggling with Meta’s chaotic AI strategy, according to sources and internal discussions reviewed by WIRED.

items.title: Donald Trump’s White House UFC Event Would Be Embarrassing Anywhere
items.summary: A Monster Energy–sponsored MMA show on the White House’s South Lawn was never going to be the height of dignity. But UFC Freedom 250 is failing to clear even the lowest bar.

items.title: Chinese Drivers Are Using Tiny Plastic Heads to Fool Tesla’s Autopilot Safeguards
items.summary: A cottage industry of celebrity figurines, blinking screens, and other DIY gadgets is helping drivers bypass Tesla's distracted-driving controls.

items.title: Chinese cybercrime operation that used AI to scam ‘hundreds of thousands of victims’ sued by Google
items.summary: The tech giant said a group called "Outsider Enterprise" used AI to scam hundreds of thousands of victims, sending 2.5 million text messages over a span of two weeks.

items.title: SpaceX IPO closes up 19% and delivers the world’s first trillionaire
items.summary: The company made its heavily anticipated debut on Friday, trading higher than its initial $135 IPO price.

items.title: SpaceX IPO: Live updates on everything you need to know
items.summary: TechCrunch has followed SpaceX's start, struggles, and successes from the early days. And we're here for what happens next too. This package of SpaceX IPO coverage includes who stands to win (and maybe some who won't), pre-IPO deals, and what's tucked inside its S-1 registration document.

items.title: Deal to end fighting would lead to Hormuz reopening, Iran says
items.summary: The deal which will pave the way for hostilities to end is close to being finalised, the US, Iran and mediators Pakistan say.

items.title: Elon Musk becomes world's first trillionaire as SpaceX soars in stock market debut
items.summary: Musk is now worth $1.11tn according to the Bloomberg rich list, while SpaceX listed on the Nasdaq stock exchange with a value of $2.2tn.

items.title: French town buries murdered child as questions mount over police failings
items.summary: The prime suspect in the killing of Lyhanna, 11, was reported to police nine months prior but never questioned.

items.title: The S&P 500 already made a big call on SpaceX stock and index fund investors need to know it
items.summary: The S&P 500 index, where trillions in retirement savings are invested, said no to the SpaceX IPO, the biggest in history. Here's what that means for portfolios.

items.title: SpaceX raising $75 billion in record-setting IPO as Nasdaq debut awaits
items.summary: SpaceX is selling 555.6 shares at $135 a piece, raising $75 billion in the largest IPO on record.

items.title: SpaceX cuts retail IPO allocation to low 20% range, source says
items.summary: The Elon Musk-led company plans to direct a percentage in the low 20s of the offering to retail buyers, according to a person familiar with the matter.

items.title: Stocks Sink in Broad AI Rout Sparked by China's DeepSeek
items.summary: U.S. stocks were mostly lower, with the Nasdaq leading declines as makers of AI infrastructure suffered steep falls, many in the double digits. Nvidia was down 16%.

items.title: Comex Gold, Silver Settle Lower
items.summary: Gold settled 1.4% lower, down two of the past three sessions, and silver fell 2.5%, down three of the past four sessions.

items.title: DeepSeek Won't Sink U.S. AI Titans

The highly anticipated initial public offering (IPO) popped 11 percent when trading officially opened on the Nasdaq, sending the company’s valuation soaring to a massive $1.96 trillion. James Comer (R-Ky.) on Friday to call several Trump administration officials in for testimony following an explosive report on the White House’s purported efforts to contain the scandal surrounding convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein. Rep. Robert Garcia (D-Calif.) pressed Comer in a letter to “take immediate steps”…

items.title: These tiny holes could change how the world cleans water
items.summary: A new nature-inspired membrane uses perfectly uniform one-nanometer pores to filter molecules with remarkable precision. The technology could transform industries such as pharmaceuticals and textiles by reducing energy consumption, improving water reuse, and delivering separation performance far beyond current filters.

items.title: Alaska’s glaciers have a startling response to rising temperatures
items.summary: Alaska’s glaciers are proving to be highly sensitive to warming temperatures. Using radar satellites to monitor more than 3,000 glaciers, researchers found that every 1°C (1.8°F) increase in average summer temperature extends glacier melting by about three weeks. The study also revealed that intense heat waves can strip away up to 28% more protective snow cover, exposing ice much earlier than normal and accelerating ice loss.

items.title: Scientists discover a surprising cancer link to Alzheimer’s disease
items.summary: Researchers discovered that mutations linked to blood cancers may help trigger Alzheimer’s disease by creating overly inflammatory immune cells in the brain. The unexpected finding could lead to new blood-based screening methods and potential treatments borrowed from cancer medicine.

items.title: A popular sunscreen ingredient can finally be sold in the United States
items.summary: The FDA will allow bemotrizinol in sunscreen. The chemical is long-lasting and defends against solar radiation that ages skin.

items.title: A new method could spot fentanyl variants no one has cataloged yet
items.summary: Researchers used machine learning to help predict chemical signatures for over 1 billion possible fentanyls, including variants never seen before.
